a single inference call can cost half a cent, and card networks have no way to charge for that. most cards carry a flat fee near thirty cents per transaction, so anything below a few dollars loses money before it clears. x402 changes that math by settling in stablecoins directly over http. @solana's 400ms finality and $0.00025 transaction costs make it an efficient settlement layer for x402 payments, and the only cost on @base is a fraction of a cent as well. 

as of march 2026, x402 has processed over 119 million transactions on base and 35 million on solana, handling roughly $600 million in annualized volume with zero protocol fees. that volume shows agents are already paying per request instead of per subscription, and the rails are holding up under real load. 

agents making thousands of calls a day need pricing that matches their behavior. per-request billing only works when the cost of moving money drops well below the cost of the resource being bought. ampersend sits on top of that settlement layer, enforcing spend limits and compliance checks so every payment clears within policy before it executes.

My thoughts on Agentic Commerce

- agents will obviously spend billions online
- AI + crypto finally make sense
- online first becomes agent first
- we are seeing exponential growth
- Stripe/Robinhood/Cloudflare are getting into it
- APIs are the first clear use-case
- uniquely enabled by stablecoins
- people trust agents more and more
- usage based pricing is just better
- every agent will need a wallet
- x402/mpp kill API keys
- agent identity is still unsolved
- AgentCash solved discovery
- everyone is building for agents now
- your agent can buy flights, domains, flowers
- if you are in crypto pivot to agentic commerce
- AX (agent experience) comes before UX
- still super early
